detail: DuBois has been continuously EAS-dependent since deregulation; Southern Airways Express holds a four-year DOT contract renewed 2024 for 19 weekly flights each to PIT and IAD; first flight was Allegheny Airlines June 1, 1960.

detail: Altoona lost nonstop Pittsburgh service July 2007 and Washington-Dulles September 2014; the DOT approved a nearly $28 million four-year Alternate EAS funding agreement for Contour Airlines service to Charlotte (CLT) beginning October 1, 2024.
source: Altoona-Blair County Airport Wikipedia; altoonamirror.com Jul 2024

Lancaster Airport Authority website; current EAS service to PIT
Lancaster Airport Wikipedia; Allegheny/All American Airways historical service

detail: Lancaster traffic peaked mid-1970s with 15 commercial flights daily to Pittsburgh, Philadelphia and Baltimore; the airport lost scheduled service multiple times and relies on federal EAS subsidy; categorized as general aviation airport by NPIAS based on 2008 enplanements under 2,500.
source: Lancaster Airport Wikipedia; lancasteronline.com; witf.org Sep 2025
